subject: physics
question_text: In a particular system, the unit of length, mass and time are chosen to be 10cm, 10g and 0.1 sec respectively. The unit of force in this system will be equivalent to:
solution_text: Using n₂ = n₁[M₁/M₂]¹[L₁/L₂]¹[T₁/T₂]⁻², we get Unit of Force = [10g]·[10cm]·[0.1s]⁻² = (10⁻² kg)·(10⁻¹ m)·(10⁻¹ s)⁻² = 10⁻¹ N = 0.1 N.

subject: physics
question_text: If momentum (p), area (A) and time (T) are taken to be fundamental quantities, then energy has the dimensional formula:
solution_text: Let E = k · p^a · A^b · T^c. Substituting dimensions: [ML²T⁻²] = [MLT⁻¹]^a · [L²]^b · [T]^c = M^a L^(a+2b) T^(-a+c). Comparing powers gives a=1, b=1/2, c=-1. Therefore, [E] = [p A¹/² T⁻¹].

subject: physics
question_text: If speed of light (c), acceleration due to gravity (g) and pressure (P) are taken as fundamental units, the dimensions of gravitational constant (G) are:
solution_text: Let G = c^x · g^y · P^z. Equating dimensions: [M⁻¹L³T⁻²] = [LT⁻¹]^x · [LT⁻²]^y · [ML⁻¹T⁻²]^z. Solving the simultaneous linear equations yields z = -1, y = 2, and x = 0. Thus, G = [c⁰ g² P⁻¹].

subject: physics
question_text: The number of significant figures in 0.06900 is:
solution_text: Leading zeros are not significant. Trailing zeros after a decimal point are significant. Therefore, 6, 9, 0, and 0 are significant (4 figures).

subject: physics
question_text: A physical quantity A is related to four observations a, b, c and d as follows: A = (a²b³) / (c√d). The percentage errors of measurement in a, b, c and d are 1%, 3%, 2% and 2% respectively. What is the percentage error in the measurement of quantity A?
solution_text: Relative error ΔA/A = 2(Δa/a) + 3(Δb/b) + (Δc/c) + 0.5(Δd/d). Substituting percentage errors: 2(1%) + 3(3%) + 2% + 0.5(2%) = 2% + 9% + 2% + 1% = 14%.

subject: physics
question_text: Error in the measurement of radius of a sphere is 1%. The error in the calculated value of its volume is:
solution_text: Volume of a sphere V = (4/3)πr³. The fractional error in volume is ΔV/V = 3(Δr/r) = 3 × 1% = 3%.
